Syllabus approved by Feis Registrar of An Comhairle Uladh:
Entries will open on Friday the 4th of June at 8 am.Please go to the following website to enter and make
payment, this can be done by a teacher or parent.
Competition entries will be capped and competitions with 6 or more boys will have a separate boys
(Includes marks, 2 solo results and door entry for 1 parent/guardian.)
(Includes a placement for each dance and door entry for one parent/guardian.)
The nearest hotel to the dancing venue is the Balmoral Hotel, Blacks Road, Dunmurry. The hotel is 0.5
mile away and is within walking distance. If you wish to avail of rates below, please email
bernadette@balmoralhotelbelfast.co.uk to make your reservation. Safe and secure parking is included
in the rates. Some presentations / events may take place in the hotel in the evenings. The hotel has two
restaurants providing a full range of food offerings. The hotel website is
https://www.balmoralhotelbelfast.co.uk. A limited number of rooms are available.
Syllabus approved by Feis Registrar of An Comhairle Uladh:
Belfast Championships will run in accordance with NI government guidelines, which can be found in full
on the NI Direct website at: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/coronavirus-covid-19.
By entering the feis, you agree to:
• Sanitise your hands often - several stations will be available in the halls
• Stay at least 2 metres apart from those not in your group
• Wear a face covering if you are not competing
• Comply with all contact tracing procedures
• Arrive at your assigned time and be prepared to dance at your assigned times
• Follow the one-way system which will be clearly marked
• Online entry and payment eliminates the need for cash handling on the day
• Temperature checks at the door on entry
• All surfaces will be sanitised by cleaners between each group entering the hall
• Toilets will be sanitised regularly throughout the day by cleaners
• Strictly only one accompanying adult per dancer - no exceptions
• If there are falls or sickness on stage, only the accompanying adult will be permitted to assist
• No contact in presentations of awards
• Risk assessment required by law has been carried out and will be on display at the feis
If you have a temperature, sore throat, headache, loss of taste, loss of smell, etc. you should NOT
attend the feis. Please note that anyone who refuses to comply with the rules of the feis will be asked to
leave, in the interest of the safety of all attendees of the feis.
Syllabus approved by Feis Registrar of An Comhairle Uladh:
3. Entries only accepted from teachers registered with An Coimisiún le Rincí Gaelacha.
4. In Bun Grád and Tús Grád, up to and including under 12, make-up and tan are not
permitted. In Mean Grád, up to and including under 10, make-up and tan are not permitted.
5. In Bun Grád and Tús Grád, dancers may only wear class costume or day clothes. No solo
6. Bun Grád competitions are confined to dancers who have never won 1st place, or have been
re-graded, in that particular dance in any grade or age. Basic steps must be performed.
7. Tús Grád competitions are confined to dancers who have never won 1st place, or have been
re-graded, in that particular dance in Tús Grád or higher.
8. Mean Grád competitions are confined to dancers who have never won 1st place in that
particular dance in Mean Grád or higher.
9. No block/en point (stationary or moving) will be allowed for all ages up to and including
10. Music will be played at the following speeds:
Bun Grád: Reel 122-124, Single Jig 124, Light Jig 116.
Tús Grád: Reel 116-118, Single Jig 124, Light Jig 116, Slip Jig 120, Heavy Jig 82.
Mean Grád: Reel 113, Single Jig 124, Slip Jig 113, Heavy Jig 73, Hornpipe 113.
Traditional Set Dances: Blackbird 144, St. Patrick’s Day 94, Job of Journeywork 138,
Garden of Daises 138, King of the Fairies 130, Three Sea Captains 96, Jockey to the Fair
11. Dancers will be re-graded as follows: 5-30 competitors - 1 dancer re-graded, 31-50
competitors - 2 dancers re-graded, 51+ competitors - 3 dancers re-graded. All re-graded
dancers will receive a trophy as per first place.
12. Any form of unauthorised photography which has the capability to capture a dancer’s
image whilst in motion, using electronic or manual means e.g. mobile phone, standard
camera, video camcorder, with or without flash enhancement, is strictly prohibited.
13. Any parent who would not like their child to appear in photos taken by the feis committee
should make the feis organiser aware prior to results being announced.
14. Any objections must be lodged in writing within one hour of result, accompanied by fee of
15. The feis committee will not accept any responsibility for personal injuries or articles lost at
Syllabus approved by Feis Registrar of An Comhairle Uladh:
16. The feis committee reserves the right to transfer competitions/results to a different stage
or to alternate competitions if it becomes necessary in order to help the feis run smoothly.
The committee reserves the right to use a relief adjudicator.
17. The feis committee reserve the right to refuse feis entries or entrance into the feis.

Syllabus approved by Feis Registrar of An Comhairle Uladh:
In advance of attending the feis we require you to carefully consider the following agreement as your/your child’s
entry will be determined on the signing of same.
I am aware that the World Health Organisation and other governmental and health authorities have declared
Covid-19 a pandemic. I am aware that social distancing is to be practiced and that any increased social contact
during this pandemic poses an increased risk of transmissions which could cause injury, illness or death.
I confirm that I (or on behalf of the participant) am in good health and have not travelled internationally in the
past 14 days and will inform my teachers, who will inform the feis organisers, should they plan to travel abroad.
ACKNOWLEDEGEGMNT AND VOLUNTARY ASSUMPTION OF RISK
I am aware that other persons may attend the feis and may have been exposed to the Covid-19 virus. I am aware
of the risks, dangers and hazards associated with attending the feis at this time and I (or on behalf of the
participant) freely accept and fully assume all such risks, dangers and hazards and the possibility of personal
injury, illness or loss resulting there from. I am aware that I (or on behalf of the participant) may require first aid,
which may require ‘hands-on’ care and social distancing may not be possible in this instance.
I agree to waive any and all claims that I now or in the future may have against the feis organisers, any of their
directors, employees, contractors, assigns, and/or successors (the“releasees”) and to release the releasees from
any and all liability for any injury, illness or loss may suffer as a result of attendance at the feis due to Covid-19. I
agree the feis organisers have carried out a thorough risk assessment prior to the running of the feis and have put
in place all measures to negate any risk of the virus being spread and/or contracted during same.
I agree to hold harmless and indemnify the releasees from any and all liability for any injury, illness or loss to from
attendance at the feis as the case may be, due to Covid-19. I confirm that I have read and understood this
agreement and I am aware that, by signing this agreement, I am waiving certain legal rights which I may have
I CONFIRM THAT I AM VOLUNTARILY SIGNING THIS AGREEMENT.

This is to certify that I, as parent/guardian, with legal responsibility for this participant, have read and explained
the provisions in this waiver/release to my child including the risks of presence and participation and his/her
